name: Incident Response
description: Incident response restores service and learns from failures: an alert is triaged and assigned a severity, an incident is declared and responders assembled, the root cause is investigated, a mitigation applied, and service confirmed restored; a blameless postmortem then captures action items so the failure mode is prevented.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Alert / incident detected"]
    N2["Triage + assign severity"]
    N3["Declare incident"]
    N4["Assemble responders"]
    N5["Investigate root cause"]
    N6{"Mitigation found?"}
    N7["Gather more data"]
    N8["Apply mitigation"]
    N9{"Service restored?"}
    N10["Postmortem + actions"]
    N11["Resolved + learnings"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 -->|no| N7
    N7 -->|investigate| N5
    N6 -->|yes| N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 -->|no| N5
    N9 -->|yes| N10
    N10 --> N11

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N11 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Site Reliability Engineering: How Google Runs Production Systems
sources.authors: Beyer, B.; Jones, C.; Petoff, J.; Murphy, N. R.

sources.title: Blameless PostMortems and a Just Culture

notes: Source-grounded extraction (v2): node content derived from cited protocol literature with per-node provenance; topology reflects the actual procedure.

flowchartStandard.name: source_grounded_extraction_v2
flowchartStandard.sourceGrounding: Nodes, decisions, branches, and loops were extracted from the cited sources; each node carries a sourceRef to its supporting citation. Awaiting human spot-check before verified=true.
